churches.name: Mellifont Abbey
churches.notes: The 1142 'Mother House'. Study for Octagon lavabo geometry.

churches.name: Boyle Abbey
churches.notes: Exceptional transition from Romanesque to Gothic. High structural integrity in nave.

churches.name: Jerpoint Abbey
churches.notes: Renowned for its 15th-century cloister sculptures and plan clarity.

churches.name: Tintern Abbey (Parva)
churches.notes: Partial reuse as a residence in the 16th century; a historical 'adaptive reuse' case.

churches.name: Duiske Abbey
churches.notes: One of the few medieval Cistercian naves still in active liturgical use today.

churches.name: Holy Cross Abbey
churches.notes: Major 15th-century reconstruction. Exemplary rib vaulting and shrine architecture.

churches.name: Baltinglass Abbey
churches.notes: Strategically located valley site; study for early Cistercian masonry.

churches.name: Bective Abbey
churches.notes: Fortified monastic appearance; study for internal defensive courtyard volumes.

churches.name: Grey Abbey
churches.notes: Anglo-Norman influence on Cistercian plan. Early English Gothic windows.

churches.name: Dunbrody Abbey
churches.notes: Massive cruciform shell; one of the largest Cistercian ruins in Ireland.

churches.name: Corcomroe Abbey

churches.name: Abbeyknockmoy
churches.notes: Rare surviving medieval wall paintings (The Three Dead Kings).

churches.name: Hore Abbey
churches.notes: Last Cistercian foundation; overlooks Rock of Cashel. Proximity analysis.

churches.name: Mount Melleray Abbey
churches.notes: Trappist revival site. Modern monastic complex with large-scale industrial farming hulls.

churches.name: Mount St. Joseph Roscrea

churches.name: Glenstal Abbey
churches.notes: Norman-revival castle converted to monastery. Study for residential/educational reuse.

churches.name: Kylemore Abbey
churches.notes: Iconic neo-Gothic estate. Benedictine adaptation of a private domestic palace.

churches.name: Fore Abbey

churches.name: Silverstream Priory
churches.notes: Benedictine Monks of Perpetual Adoration. Traditional liturgical hub.

churches.name: St. Mary's Glencairn
churches.notes: Cistercian tradition for women. Agri-monastic production site.

churches.name: St. Brigid's Drumcong

churches.name: Donnybrook Sacred Heart
churches.notes: Imposing brick modernist shell; study for sculptural vertical light.

churches.name: Annunciation Finglas

churches.name: Creeslough St. Michael's

churches.name: St. Aengus Burt
churches.notes: Circular 'Grianan' inspired plan. National design masterpiece.

churches.name: Church of the Redeemer Dundalk
churches.notes: Concrete 'tent' structure; study for hyperbolic paraboloid roofs.

churches.name: St. Conal's Glenties
churches.notes: Flat-roofed geometric glass and steel study by McCormick.

churches.name: Our Lady of Victories Ballymun
churches.notes: Brutalist era anchor; massive interior clear-span for reuse.

churches.name: St. Paul's Ayrfield
churches.notes: Timber-lined radial ceiling; exceptional acoustic/thermal study.

churches.name: St. John the Evangelist Ballinteer

churches.name: St. Kevin's Harrington St

churches.notes: Conversion of SS Michael & Johns; performance space adaptation.

churches.name: St. Mary’s Castleblayney

churches.name: St. Mary’s Killarney

churches.name: St. Colman’s Cathedral Cobh
churches.notes: Dramatic verticality on steep topographical slopes.

churches.notes: Historic crypt utility and structural transition.

churches.notes: Study for light-and-shadow on Kilkenny limestone.
